title: Carbohydrate periodization improves 40-km time-trial performance in trained cyclists: a randomized crossover trial
abstract: Twenty trained male cyclists completed a 3-week randomized crossover trial comparing a periodized carbohydrate strategy (sleep-low, train-low on selected sessions) against high-carbohydrate availability. The periodized arm improved 40-km time-trial power by 3.1% (p=0.01) and increased fat oxidation rate at submaximal intensity, with no decrement in high-intensity session quality. Findings support strategic manipulation of carbohydrate availability within a competition build.

title: Skeletal muscle mitochondrial biogenesis signalling in sedentary mice following acute nitrate ingestion
abstract: We examined PGC-1alpha and downstream mitochondrial biogenesis markers in the soleus of sedentary C57BL/6 mice after a single oral dose of sodium nitrate. Western blot analysis showed a transient increase in phosphorylated AMPK at 2 hours post-ingestion but no change in citrate synthase activity at 24 hours. The mechanistic, animal-model nature of the work limits direct translation to human athletic performance.
